proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative effect).
stage_iv=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0.
sex_female is associated with a difference in pfs_months versus male patients.

analyses.code: from scipy.stats import linregress; linregress(df['ecog_ps'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Univariate linear regression of pfs_months on ecog_ps: slope = -1.190 months per ECOG point (p<1e-300). Each one-point increase in ECOG performance status is associated with ~1.19 months shorter PFS.

analyses.code: from scipy.stats import linregress; linregress(df['stage_iv'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Univariate slope of pfs_months on stage_iv = -1.352 months (p<1e-300). Stage IV patients have ~1.35 months shorter PFS than non-stage IV.

analyses.code: from scipy.stats import linregress; linregress(df['sex_female'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Slope on sex_female = -0.006 months (p=0.77). No detectable sex effect on PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative effect, as is typical in oncology).
right_sided_primary=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than left-sided primary tumors.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate slope = +0.177 months per year of age (p<1e-300). Direction is OPPOSITE to the proposed hypothesis: older patients have LONGER PFS in this cohort. Robust across ECOG and stage strata (slope ~+0.16-0.18 in every stratum).
Slope = -0.305 months (p=5.2e-46). Right-sided primary is associated with ~0.31 months shorter PFS, consistent with hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: kras_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than KRAS wild-type.
braf_v600e=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than BRAF wild-type.
nras_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than NRAS wild-type.
msi_high=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than microsatellite-stable patients (better prognosis in MSI-H).

analyses.result_summary: Slope = -0.327 months (p=8.5e-56). KRAS-mutant patients have ~0.33 months shorter PFS, consistent with hypothesis.
Slope = -0.228 months (p=3.7e-6). BRAF V600E mutant patients have ~0.23 months shorter PFS, consistent with hypothesis.
Slope = +0.221 months (p=2.3e-4). NRAS-mutant patients have slightly LONGER PFS, opposite to hypothesis. Effect small and likely not robust to confounders.
Slope = -0.019 months (p=0.68). No detectable PFS difference between MSI-high and MSS patients in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Lower albumin_g_dl is associated with shorter pfs_months (positive slope of PFS on albumin).
Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).

analyses.result_summary: Slope = +0.465 months per g/dL of albumin (p=3.2e-115). Each 1 g/dL increase in albumin corresponds to ~0.47 months longer PFS.
Slope = -0.0054 months per ng/mL (p=3.1e-17). Higher CEA is associated with shorter PFS.
Slope = -0.073 months per percentage point of weight loss (p=2.2e-167). Greater weight loss is strongly associated with shorter PFS.
Slope = -0.00032 months per U/L (p=0.013). Higher LDH is weakly associated with shorter PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher nlr (neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
her2_amplified=1 is associated with a different pfs_months than HER2-non-amplified.
ntrk_fusion=1 is associated with a different pfs_months than NTRK-non-fused.

analyses.result_summary: Slope = -0.002 months per mg/L (p=0.065). Trend toward shorter PFS with higher CRP, but not significant at alpha=0.05.
Slope = +0.005 months per NLR unit (p=0.29). No detectable association in univariate analysis.
Slope = -0.056 months (p=0.35). No detectable PFS effect of HER2 amplification status.
Slope = +0.040 months (p=0.78). No detectable PFS effect (only ~250 patients with NTRK fusion).

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_cetuximab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than untreated.
treatment_bevacizumab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than untreated.
treatment_pembrolizumab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than untreated.
treatment_encorafenib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than untreated.
treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than untreated.
treatment_regorafenib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than untreated.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS on cetuximab = 4.285 vs off = 4.323 months (diff = -0.038, p=0.088). No detectable benefit; trend is slightly worse.
Mean PFS on bevacizumab = 4.301 vs off = 4.320 (diff = -0.019, p=0.354). No detectable benefit.
Mean PFS on pembrolizumab = 4.316 vs off = 4.311 (diff = +0.005, p=0.86). No detectable benefit.
Mean PFS on encorafenib = 4.316 vs off = 4.311 (diff = +0.005, p=0.87). No detectable benefit.
Mean PFS on trastuzumab+tucatinib = 4.271 vs off = 4.315 (diff = -0.044, p=0.24). No detectable benefit.
Mean PFS on regorafenib = 5.089 vs off = 4.117 (diff = +0.972 months, p=1.7e-216). LARGE main effect: regorafenib adds ~1 month PFS in the marginal pool.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months specifically in patients with msi_high=1, with positive interaction (T*B coefficient > 0).

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ T + B + T:B; subset msi_high==1 t-test
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient pembrolizumab*msi_high = +0.002, p=0.99. Within MSI-high (n=2513): mean PFS on pembro=4.299 vs off=4.292 (diff=+0.007, p=0.96). Hypothesis REFUTED — pembrolizumab shows no benefit in MSI-high patients in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_encorafenib improves pfs_months specifically in patients with braf_v600e=1, with positive interaction.
Encorafenib + cetuximab combination further extends pfs_months in BRAF V600E-mutant patients vs neither/either alone.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient encorafenib*braf_v600e = -0.141, p=0.38. Within BRAF V600E (n=2272): mean PFS on encorafenib = 3.979 vs off = 4.108 (diff = -0.129, p=0.33). Hypothesis REFUTED — encorafenib shows no benefit (numerical decrement) in BRAF V600E patients.

analyses.code: subset braf_v600e==1; combo (enc=1 & cet=1) vs other
analyses.result_summary: BRAF V600E with encorafenib+cetuximab combo (n=58): mean PFS = 4.151 vs other (n=2214): 4.093 (diff = +0.058, p=0.84). No detectable benefit of the combination.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib improves pfs_months specifically in patients with her2_amplified=1, with positive interaction.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient trastuzumab_tucatinib*her2_amplified = +0.063, p=0.78. Within HER2-amplified (n=1504): on=4.273 vs off=4.256 (diff=+0.017, p=0.93). Hypothesis REFUTED — no detectable benefit in HER2-amplified patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_cetuximab improves pfs_months specifically in patients who are kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 (RAS/BRAF wild-type, left-sided primary).

analyses.code: subset kras=0 & nras=0 & braf=0 & right=0; ttest cetuximab on/off
analyses.result_summary: RAS/BRAF wild-type left-sided subgroup (n=16443): on cetuximab = 4.617 vs off = 4.684 (diff = -0.067, p=0.12). Hypothesis REFUTED — cetuximab does not improve PFS even in the textbook subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The regorafenib effect on pfs_months is modified by kras_mutation: regorafenib improves PFS in KRAS wild-type but has reduced/no benefit in KRAS-mutant.
The regorafenib effect on pfs_months is modified by braf_v600e: regorafenib improves PFS in BRAF wild-type but has reduced/no benefit in BRAF V600E.
The regorafenib effect on pfs_months is modified by right_sided_primary: regorafenib improves PFS in left-sided but has reduced/no benefit in right-sided primary.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ T + B + T:B for regorafenib x kras
analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = -1.629, p=7.7e-226. Stratified: in KRAS-WT regorafenib effect = +1.656 months; in KRAS-mutant effect = +0.027 months. Hypothesis SUPPORTED with extreme magnitude — KRAS mutation completely abolishes the regorafenib benefit.
Interaction beta = -1.183, p=6.1e-23. In BRAF-WT regorafenib effect = +1.027; in BRAF V600E effect = -0.156. Hypothesis SUPPORTED — BRAF V600E abolishes the regorafenib benefit.
Interaction beta = -1.454, p=4.1e-168. In left-sided regorafenib effect = +1.480; in right-sided effect = +0.026. Hypothesis SUPPORTED — right-sided primary abolishes the regorafenib benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Regorafenib improves pfs_months substantially and ONLY in patients who are simultaneously kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 (KRAS-WT, BRAF-WT, left-sided); outside this triple-positive subgroup it has no effect.

analyses.code: stratify on kras x braf x right; ttest regorafenib in each cell
analyses.result_summary: In the joint subgroup (kras=0 & braf=0 & right=0, n=17,372): regorafenib raises PFS from 4.117 to 6.875 (diff = +2.758, p<1e-300). Outside this subgroup (n=32,628): on=4.147 vs off=4.117 (diff=+0.030, p=0.29). Hypothesis SUPPORTED: regorafenib effect entirely concentrated in the KRAS-WT/BRAF-WT/left-sided subgroup; null elsewhere.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The regorafenib effect on pfs_months is further modified by cea_ng_ml: lower CEA enhances and higher CEA attenuates the benefit.
The regorafenib effect on pfs_months is modified by ecog_ps (effect smaller in higher-ECOG patients).

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ T + CEA + T:CEA, full sample
analyses.result_summary: T:CEA interaction beta = -0.0253, p=8.7e-58. Within the KRAS-WT/BRAF-WT/left-sided subgroup, the T:CEA interaction is even more pronounced: beta = -0.065, p<1e-100. Hypothesis SUPPORTED — higher CEA attenuates regorafenib benefit.

analyses.code: stratify by ecog_ps; ttest regorafenib on/off
analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib effect by ECOG: ecog=0 diff=+0.925, ecog=1 diff=+0.995, ecog=2 diff=+0.896. Effect essentially independent of ECOG. Hypothesis REFUTED — ECOG is not a meaningful modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the KRAS-WT/BRAF-WT/left-sided subgroup, the regorafenib benefit on pfs_months is concentrated at low CEA (cea_ng_ml < ~5 ng/mL) and disappears above ~5 ng/mL.

analyses.code: scan thresholds 1..20 ng/mL within k0/b0/r0 subset
analyses.result_summary: Threshold scan within KRAS-WT/BRAF-WT/left-sided: at CEA<5 regorafenib diff = +5.013 months (n_low=9326); at CEA>=5 regorafenib diff = +0.005 months (n_high=8046). The threshold is sharp at ~5 ng/mL: below it regorafenib roughly DOUBLES PFS, above it has no effect. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Regorafenib increases pfs_months ONLY in patients who simultaneously satisfy all four predicates: kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND cea_ng_ml<5; outside this fourfold subgroup the regorafenib effect is null.

analyses.result_summary: Inside subgroup (n=9326): mean PFS on regorafenib = 9.101 vs off = 4.088 (diff = +5.013, t=90.4, p<1e-300). Outside subgroup (n=40,674): on=4.147 vs off=4.117 (diff = +0.025, p=0.33). The regorafenib treatment effect is confined to this 4-feature subgroup: KRAS-WT, BRAF-WT, left-sided primary, CEA<5 ng/mL.

proposed_hypotheses.text: nras_mutation modifies the regorafenib effect (within the KRAS-WT/BRAF-WT/left-sided subgroup, NRAS-mutant patients still derive benefit similar to NRAS-WT).

analyses.result_summary: Within KRAS-WT/BRAF-WT/left-sided: NRAS-WT (n=16,443) regorafenib diff = +2.749, p<1e-300; NRAS-mutant (n=929) regorafenib diff = +2.906, p=7.1e-25. Effect indistinguishable; NRAS is NOT a useful modifier (kras/nras/braf are mutually exclusive, so NRAS-mutant patients remain in the responsive group).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity exists for treatment_cetuximab, treatment_bevacizumab, treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_encorafenib, or treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib with respect to any continuous lab feature (significant T:F interaction at p<0.001).

analyses.code: for T in {cetux,bev,pembro,enco,tras}: OLS pfs ~ T + F + T:F over all 9 continuous features
analyses.result_summary: Of 5 treatments x 9 continuous features = 45 interaction tests, NONE reached p<0.001. Strongest hint was a small bevacizumab x ECOG=1 stratum effect (-0.056, p=0.04). Hypothesis REFUTED — no continuous-feature heterogeneity for these five treatments.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity exists for any non-regorafenib treatment with respect to any of the 8 binary features (kras, nras, braf, msi, her2, ntrk, right-sided, stage IV) at p<0.05.
T-learner CATE estimates from gradient-boosted regression trees show meaningful heterogeneity (CATE std > 0.5 months) for at least one non-regorafenib treatment.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ T + B + T:B over 5 treatments x 8 binary features = 40 tests
analyses.result_summary: No pre-specified interaction reached p<0.001. The most extreme were bevacizumab x braf (beta=-0.228, p=0.022; small effect) and pembrolizumab x right_sided (beta=-0.116, p=0.05). After Bonferroni correction (40 tests), nothing remains significant. Hypothesis REFUTED at study-wide alpha.

analyses.code: T-learner with GradientBoostingRegressor for each non-regorafenib T
analyses.result_summary: CATE distributions: cetuximab std=0.14, bevacizumab std=0.14, pembrolizumab std=0.16, encorafenib std=0.18, trastuzumab+tucatinib std=0.22. All <0.5. Top features by tree importance are dominated by age_years (likely overfitting noise). Hypothesis REFUTED — no meaningful CATE heterogeneity for these treatments.

proposed_hypotheses.text: right_sided_primary x kras_mutation interact on pfs_months (the negative effects do not simply add).
albumin_g_dl x stage_iv interact on pfs_months (low albumin is more harmful in stage IV).
weight_loss_pct_6mo x ecog_ps interact on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = +0.452, p=1.7e-25. Right-sided alone -0.494, KRAS alone -0.486, but joint effect = -0.494-0.486+0.452 = -0.528. The two negatives are largely sub-additive: KRAS mutation adds little additional harm in already-right-sided tumors.
Interaction beta = +0.032, p=0.41. No detectable interaction between albumin and stage IV.
Interaction beta = +0.002, p=0.59. No detectable weight loss x ECOG interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a full multivariable OLS controlling for all features and treatments, regorafenib remains significantly associated with longer PFS while other treatments remain non-significant.
After multivariable adjustment, age_years retains a positive coefficient on pfs_months (older = longer PFS), in contradiction to typical oncology priors.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ all features + all treatments
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted treatment coefficients (multivariable, n=50,000, R^2=0.86): regorafenib +0.943 (p<1e-300); cetuximab -0.011 (p=0.19); bevacizumab +0.003 (p=0.73); pembrolizumab +0.0003 (p=0.98); encorafenib +0.006 (p=0.63); trastuzumab+tucatinib +0.026 (p=0.07). Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

Adjusted age coefficient = +0.176 months per year (p<1e-300). Direction unchanged after full adjustment for ECOG, stage, biomarkers, labs, and treatments. Hypothesis SUPPORTED — age is positively associated with PFS in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Stage IV combined with ECOG=2 yields markedly shorter PFS than the additive model would predict.
Patients with both stage_iv=1 AND ecog_ps>=1 have mean pfs_months below 4 months, while patients with stage_iv=0 AND ecog_ps=0 have mean pfs_months above 5 months.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction S:E beta = +0.040, p=0.39. No detectable super-additive effect between stage IV and ECOG. The two prognostic axes act additively.
Mean PFS: stage=0/ecog=0 = 5.84; stage=1/ecog>=1 = 3.52. Hypothesis SUPPORTED: PFS in best-prognosis cell exceeds 5 months and in worst-prognosis cell falls below 4 months. Difference of ~2.3 months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment-treatment interactions exist (e.g., regorafenib+bevacizumab combination has synergistic or antagonistic effect).

analyses.code: for each pair T1,T2: OLS pfs ~ T1 + T2 + T1:T2
analyses.result_summary: All 15 pairwise treatment interaction tests had p>0.01. No detectable synergy or antagonism between any two treatments at study-wide alpha.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A T-learner tree on the regorafenib treatment effect identifies kras_mutation, braf_v600e, right_sided_primary, and cea_ng_ml as the dominant treatment-effect modifiers (confirming the joint subgroup).

analyses.code: GradientBoostingRegressor as T-learner; DecisionTreeRegressor on CATE
analyses.result_summary: Decision-tree on regorafenib CATE finds primary splits on right_sided_primary, kras_mutation, braf_v600e, and cea_ng_ml — the same four features identified by interaction tests. CATE within the joint subgroup ~+5 months; CATE outside ~0 months. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within MSI-high patients, pembrolizumab benefit might be detectable in left-sided primary tumors (left-sided MSI-H) compared to right-sided MSI-H.
Within MSI-high patients, pembrolizumab benefit might be detectable in stage IV patients specifically.

analyses.code: subset msi_high==1; ttest pembro on/off by right_sided
analyses.result_summary: MSI-H left-sided (n=1618): pembro on=4.468, off=4.358 (diff=+0.109, p=0.53). MSI-H right-sided (n=895): on=4.143, off=4.339 (diff=-0.197, p=0.29). Neither slice shows benefit; hypothesis REFUTED.
MSI-H stage IV (n=1404): pembro on=4.116, off=4.188 (diff=-0.072, p=0.66). No benefit. Hypothesis REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL refined treatment-effect-heterogeneity hypothesis for pfs_months: treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months by approximately +5 months EXCLUSIVELY in patients with kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND cea_ng_ml<5; outside this 4-predicate subgroup the regorafenib effect is indistinguishable from zero.

None of treatment_cetuximab, treatment_bevacizumab, treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_encorafenib, or treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib has a detectable beneficial effect on pfs_months in any subgroup defined by demographic, clinical, biomarker, or laboratory features in this cohort, even within their canonical biomarker-matched subpopulations (MSI-H, BRAF V600E, HER2-amplified, RAS/BRAF-WT left-sided).

In a final multivariable model the strongest prognostic features for longer pfs_months are (in descending |effect|) ecog_ps (-1.17/level), stage_iv (-1.36), albumin_g_dl (+0.47/g/dL), kras_mutation (-0.35), braf_v600e (-0.36), right_sided_primary (-0.29), age_years (+0.18/yr), weight_loss_pct_6mo (-0.076/%), and cea_ng_ml (-0.005/ng/mL).

analyses.result_summary: Inside (n=9326): regorafenib +5.013 months (p<1e-300). Outside (n=40,674): +0.025 months (p=0.33). Confirms refined subgroup is correct and complete.

Adjusted: cetuximab -0.011 (p=0.19), bevacizumab +0.003 (p=0.73), pembrolizumab 0.000 (p=0.98), encorafenib +0.006 (p=0.63), trastuzumab+tucatinib +0.026 (p=0.07). All negligible. Subgroup-targeted tests in canonical biomarker matches were also null. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ all features + all treatments
analyses.result_summary: All listed features remain highly significant (p<1e-50 each) in the full multivariable model with R^2=0.86. Direction and magnitude of the listed coefficients confirmed.
